title: The Great Supper
subtitle: A man prepares an eternal feast, and the invited make their excuses

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the world fall away.
You who come to the supper of God:
Love not the world, nor the things that are in the world.
gospel: A certain man made a great supper,
And he sent his servant at the hour of the supper
to say to them that were invited, that they should come,
for now all things are ready.
And they began all at once to make excuse.
The first said: I have bought a farm,
and I must needs go out and see it.
And another said: I have bought five yoke of oxen,
and I go to try them.
And another said: I have married a wife,
Many are called, but few come.
behold: Consider: a certain man — that is, Christ, true man and true God —
the refreshment of glory and of eternal life,
which the Lord prepared from eternity for holy souls.
It is called supper, because it is the last refreshment:
for as the supper is made ready when the day declines,
after which no further meal follows,
so eternal life is given after the day of this present life,
And it is called a great supper — nay, so great
that its immensity cannot rise up into the heart of man.
See that this supper consists in three things:
in the blessed vision of the divine Persons,
in the fellowship of the Angels,
and in the company of the Saints —
and all these, as it is said, are made ready.
consider: And they began all at once to make excuse,
drawing themselves back through evil works;
for they loved the earthly and bodily
more than the heavenly and spiritual.
But woe to us!
For, as Gregory says, behold, the rich man invites,
and the poor man hastens to come;
we are bidden to the banquet of God,
and we make excuse for ourselves.
The Lord mercifully invites all,
but they who will not come shut themselves out.
This excuse is a self-chosen ill-disposition:
by it some fall into pride, some into avarice, some into lust.
By the farm bought is marked dominion and pride,
for the proud wish to lord it over others.
By the oxen — which turn the earth — earthly things are signified,
the greed of those too intent on the five senses.
By the wife is marked the carnal, given to the delights of the flesh.
And all of these are unfit for the Lord's supper.
Pride bought the farm, avarice the oxen, lust the wife.
Let us then take away from our midst these vain and evil excuses,
and come to the supper, for within we shall be filled.
Let not self-exaltation hinder us;
let not pride lift us up;
let not unlawful curiosity hold us and turn us from God;
let not the pleasure of the flesh keep us from the pleasure of the heart.
Let us come, and be filled.
prayer: who, willing that all men be saved,
have prepared for all the refreshment of heavenly blessedness,
and have called many to it in many ways:
do not shut me out, wretched as I am,
from that general grace which you came to impart to all;
and grant me to trample down all pride and ambition,
the lust of the flesh and its pleasure,
that these, or any other hindrances,
may not bar the eternal banquet against me;
but, I who am poor in grace and in virtue,
mercifully lead me into that very feast. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Item, dicitur cœna magna, imo sic maxima, ut ejus immensitas in cor hominis non possit ascendere.
latinJewel.english: Again, it is called a great supper — nay, so great that its immensity cannot rise up into the heart of man.
